trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10 cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut (for reference during insertion)
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Step 5: Compute the fixed transform between handle grasp pose and nut center
# This transform will be used to align the nut during insertion
handle_to_center_translation = center_position - handle_position
handle_rotation = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])  # Convert WXYZ to XYZW for scipy
handle_to_center_rotation_offset = R.from_quat(center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]]) * handle_rotation.inv()

# Step 6: Get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion location)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Step 7: Compute desired nut center pose when inserted onto the block
inserted_center_position = block_position  # Assume center alignment
inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz = block_quaternion_wxyz  # Match block orientation

# Step 8: Back-project the required handle pose using the fixed rigid transform
# Apply inverse rotation to the translation offset
rotated_offset = handle_rotation * handle_to_center_rotation_offset.inv()
desired_handle_translation = inserted_center_position - rotated_offset.apply(handle_to_center_translation)

# Compute desired handle orientation
block_rotation = R.from_quat(block_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
desired_handle_rotation = block_rotation * handle_to_center_rotation_offset.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = desired_handle_rotation.as_quat()[[3,0,1,2]]  # Convert back to WXYZ

# Step 9: Insert the nut by moving to the computed handle pose with approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_translation, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(desired_handle_translation, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Step 10: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 11: Retract slightly and go home
retract_position = desired_handle_translation + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10 cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut (for reference during insertion)
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Step 5: Compute the fixed transform between handle grasp pose and nut center
# This transform will be used to align the nut during insertion
handle_to_center_translation = center_position - handle_position
handle_rotation = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])  # Convert WXYZ to XYZW for scipy
handle_to_center_rotation_offset = R.from_quat(center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]]) * handle_rotation.inv()

# Step 6: Get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion location)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Step 7: Compute desired nut center pose when inserted onto the block
inserted_center_position = block_position  # Assume center alignment
inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz = block_quaternion_wxyz  # Match block orientation

# Step 8: Back-project the required handle pose using the fixed rigid transform
# Apply inverse rotation to the translation offset
rotated_offset = handle_rotation * handle_to_center_rotation_offset.inv()
desired_handle_translation = inserted_center_position - rotated_offset.apply(handle_to_center_translation)

# Compute desired handle orientation
block_rotation = R.from_quat(block_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
desired_handle_rotation = block_rotation * handle_to_center_rotation_offset.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = desired_handle_rotation.as_quat()[[3,0,1,2]]  # Convert back to WXYZ

# Step 9: Insert the nut by moving to the computed handle pose with approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_translation, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(desired_handle_translation, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Step 10: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 11: Retract slightly and go home
retract_position = desired_handle_translation + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)
goto_home_joint_position()
